items.verse: أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ بِأَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلْفِيلِ
items.verseEn: Have you not considered, [O Muḥammad], how your Lord dealt with the companions of the elephant?

items.verse: وَمَآ أَفَآءَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِۦ مِنْهُمْ فَمَآ أَوْجَفْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ خَيْلٍۢ وَلَا رِكَابٍۢ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُسَلِّطُ رُسُلَهُۥ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ قَدِيرٌۭ
items.verseEn: And what Allāh restored [of property] to His Messenger from them - you did not spur for it [in an expedition] any horses or camels, but Allāh gives His messengers power over whom He wills, and Allāh is over all things competent.

items.verse: وَٱلْخَيْلَ وَٱلْبِغَالَ وَٱلْحَمِيرَ لِتَرْكَبُوهَا وَزِينَةًۭ ۚ وَيَخْلُقُ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
items.verseEn: And [He created] the horses, mules and donkeys for you to ride and [as] adornment. And He creates that which you do not know.

items.verse: إِنَّمَا حَرَّمَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْمَيْتَةَ وَٱلدَّمَ وَلَحْمَ ٱلْخِنزِيرِ وَمَآ أُهِلَّ بِهِۦ لِغَيْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ فَمَنِ ٱضْطُرَّ غَيْرَ بَاغٍۢ وَلَا عَادٍۢ فَلَآ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌۭ رَّحِيمٌ
items.verseEn: He has only forbidden to you dead animals, blood, the flesh of swine, and that which has been dedicated to other than Allāh. But whoever is forced [by necessity], neither desiring [it] nor transgressing [its limit], there is no sin upon him. Indeed, Allāh is Forgiving and Merciful.

categories.name: أَفْعَالٌ تَصِفُ تَسْخِيرَ اللَّهِ وَتَهْيِئَتَهُ لِلطَّبِيعَةِ
categories.nameEn: Verbs describing Allah's control and preparation of the natural world

items.verse: ۞ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى سَخَّرَ لَكُمُ ٱلْبَحْرَ لِتَجْرِىَ ٱلْفُلْكُ فِيهِ بِأَمْرِهِۦ وَلِتَبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
items.verseEn: It is Allāh who subjected to you the sea so that ships may sail upon it by His command and that you may seek of His bounty; and perhaps you will be grateful.

items.verse: وَدَانِيَةً عَلَيْهِمْ ظِلَـٰلُهَا وَذُلِّلَتْ قُطُوفُهَا تَذْلِيلًۭا
items.verseEn: And near above them are its shades, and its [fruit] to be picked will be lowered in compliance.
And after that He spread the earth.
And [by] the earth and He who spread it

categories.name: أَفْعَالٌ تَصِفُ أَسَالِيبَ الشَّيْطَانِ فِي الْإِغْوَاءِ
categories.nameEn: Verbs describing Satan's methods of temptation and influence

items.verse: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ تَوَلَّوْا۟ مِنكُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْتَقَى ٱلْجَمْعَانِ إِنَّمَا ٱسْتَزَلَّهُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ بِبَعْضِ مَا كَسَبُوا۟ ۖ وَلَقَدْ عَفَا ٱللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ حَلِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: Indeed, those of you who turned back on the day the two armies met [at Uḥud] - it was Satan who caused them to slip because of some [blame] they had earned. But Allāh has already forgiven them. Indeed, Allāh is Forgiving and Forbearing.

items.en: I will surely take control of
items.verse: قَالَ أَرَءَيْتَكَ هَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كَرَّمْتَ عَلَىَّ لَئِنْ أَخَّرْتَنِ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ لَأَحْتَنِكَنَّ ذُرِّيَّتَهُۥٓ إِلَّا قَلِيلًۭا
items.verseEn: [Iblees] said, "Do You see this one whom You have honored above me? If You delay me [i.e., my death] until the Day of Resurrection, I will surely destroy his descendants, except for a few."

items.en: I will surely make [evil] seem appealing
items.verse: قَالَ رَبِّ بِمَآ أَغْوَيْتَنِى لَأُزَيِّنَنَّ لَهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَأُغْوِيَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ
items.verseEn: [Iblees] said, "My Lord, because You have put me in error, I will surely make [disobedience] attractive to them [i.e., mankind] on earth, and I will mislead them all

items.verse: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَا ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينَ عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ تَؤُزُّهُمْ أَزًّۭا
items.verseEn: Do you not see that We have sent the devils upon the disbelievers, inciting them [to evil] with [constant] incitement?

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ تَدُلُّ عَلَىٰ عَيْبٍ جَسَدِيٍّ أَوْ عَدَمِ ٱسْتِوَاءٍ
categories.nameEn: Words denoting a physical defect or lack of uniformity

items.verse: وَضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًۭا رَّجُلَيْنِ أَحَدُهُمَآ أَبْكَمُ لَا يَقْدِرُ عَلَىٰ شَىْءٍۢ وَهُوَ كَلٌّ عَلَىٰ مَوْلَىٰهُ أَيْنَمَا يُوَجِّههُّ لَا يَأْتِ بِخَيْرٍ ۖ هَلْ يَسْتَوِى هُوَ وَمَن يَأْمُرُ بِٱلْعَدْلِ ۙ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍۢ مُّسْتَقِيمٍۢ
items.verseEn: And Allāh presents an example of two men, one of them dumb and unable to do a thing, while he is a burden to his guardian. Wherever he directs him, he brings no good. Is he equal to one who commands justice, while he is on a straight path?

items.verse: وَرَسُولًا إِلَىٰ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ أَنِّى قَدْ جِئْتُكُم بِـَٔايَةٍۢ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ أَنِّىٓ أَخْلُقُ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلطِّينِ كَهَيْـَٔةِ ٱلطَّيْرِ فَأَنفُخُ فِيهِ فَيَكُونُ طَيْرًۢا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَأُبْرِئُ ٱلْأَكْمَهَ وَٱلْأَبْرَصَ وَأُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَأُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا تَأْكُلُونَ وَمَا تَدَّخِرُونَ فِى بُيُوتِكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةًۭ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
items.verseEn: And [make him] a messenger to the Children of Israel, [who will say], 'Indeed I have come to you with a sign from your Lord in that I design for you from clay [that which is] like the form of a bird, then I breathe into it and it becomes a bird by permission of Allāh. And I cure the blind [from birth] and the leper, and I give life to the dead - by permission of Allāh. And I inform you of what you eat and what you store in your houses. Indeed in that is a sign for you, if you are believers.

items.verse: ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنزَلَ عَلَىٰ عَبْدِهِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَلَمْ يَجْعَل لَّهُۥ عِوَجَاۜ
items.verseEn: [All] praise is [due] to Allāh, who has sent down upon His Servant [Muḥammad (ﷺ)] the Book and has not made therein any deviance.

items.verse: لَّا تَرَىٰ فِيهَا عِوَجًۭا وَلَآ أَمْتًۭا
items.verseEn: You will not see therein a depression or an elevation."

harf.hint: A conclusive proof or clear evidence; what Prophet Yusuf (AS) saw from his Lord.
harf.arabicVerse: وَلَقَدْ هَمَّتْ بِهِۦ ۖ وَهَمَّ بِهَا لَوْلَآ أَن رَّءَا بُرْهَـٰنَ رَبِّهِۦ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ لِنَصْرِفَ عَنْهُ ٱلسُّوٓءَ وَٱلْفَحْشَآءَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا ٱلْمُخْلَصِينَ
harf.verse: 12:24 — And she certainly determined [to seduce] him, and he would have inclined to her had he not seen the proof [i.e., sign] of his Lord. And thus [it was] that We should avert from him evil and immorality. Indeed, he was of Our chosen servants.

deduction.title: The Star-Gazer's Revelation
deduction.intro: In a time when celestial bodies were worshipped and idols lined the halls of power, one soul embarked on a profound intellectual and spiritual journey. Questioning the very fabric of their people's beliefs, this individual sought the one true Creator, setting a course that would lead to confrontation, exile, and the establishment of a timeless legacy.
clues: I looked to the stars, the moon, and the sun, seeking my Lord, but I knew that which sets could not be the one to worship.
My own kin was a crafter of the very idols my people revered, and I challenged this practice directly.
With an axe, I reduced a hall of false gods to rubble, leaving the largest among them untouched to expose their powerlessness.
For my defiance, I was cast into a blazing fire, a punishment meant to make an example of me.
By Allah's command, the inferno became cool and safe for me, a miracle for all to witness.
I am known as Khalilullah, the 'Friend of Allah,' and my descendants include two great lines of prophets.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Nuh
Prophet Yusuf
Prophet Ibrahim
Dhul-Qarnayn
Prophet Shu'ayb

trial.label: What was my primary confrontation?
options: Facing a great flood in an ark
Being swallowed by a giant fish
Splitting the sea with a staff
Being thrown into a fire
Being raised to the heavens alive

location.label: What did I challenge?
options: The arrogance of a wealthy treasurer
The sorcery of a court of magicians
The worship of idols and celestial bodies
Dishonesty in weights and measures
The violation of the Sabbath
location.answer: The worship of idols and celestial bodies

outcome.label: What was the immediate miraculous result?
options: My staff turned into a serpent
The fire became cool and peaceful
A spring of water gushed from the ground
A wall was built to contain a corrupting force
A nation was destroyed by a furious wind
outcome.answer: The fire became cool and peaceful

deduction.arabic: إِنِّى وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِىَ لِلَّذِى فَطَرَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ حَنِيفًۭا ۖ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ
deduction.verse: Indeed, I have turned my face [i.e., self] toward He who created the heavens and the earth, inclining toward truth, and I am not of those who associate others with Allāh." (6:79)

scramble.hint: This is the greeting that the believers will receive directly from Allah in Paradise.
scramble.verseEn: [And] "Peace," a word from a Merciful Lord.
